mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 8% year-over-year signals persistent price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise FEDFUNDS, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reducing S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 244.158 as of July 2022, representing elevated inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY > 8% and FEDFUNDS is rising, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS (total nonfarm payroll employment) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145554 thousand as of July 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite consecutive monthly PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales measured by RSAFS decline month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 472142 million dollars as of July 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or directional accuracy is below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises simultaneously, indicating both supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration that signals stagflation risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5548 and UNRATE at 4.8% as of July 2022
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with simultaneous supply and labor shocks
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise does not produce negative 5-day returns in at least 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST fall below 1.2 million units annualized, signaling a collapse in residential investment demand that cascades into reduced construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1226 thousand units as of June 2022, near threshold
regime_hint: housing market downturn with rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when HOUST < 1.2M, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate measured by FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5% in a tightening cycle while real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 remains below 2% annualized, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% and GDPC1 at 16804.8B as of April-July 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate measured by UNRATE rises above 5.0%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% as of July 2022, approaching threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter while retail sales measured by RSAFS decline month-over-month simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand destruction that signals recession onset and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 0% AND RSAFS MoM decline > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16804.8B and RSAFS at 472142M as of April-July 2022
regime_hint: recession regime with synchronized demand collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when both GDPC1 and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate testing.
